finding_results.notes: The chapter now declares its formal access model in the opening sentence and keeps subsequent material grounded in logs, statements, photographs, scripts, notes, and email metadata instead of roaming into outsider biography or interior reaction.
The final count now reconciles on the page: 27 across floors one through three, 2 in the mechanical sublevel corridor, and 1 at the bottom of the west stairwell, for 30 total.
The relay is now described only as inbound. Families send messages into the building and wait for post-unseal contact, with no implication that researchers transmitted replies out through the relay.
The copied calibration line is replaced with new fragmentary terminal text that preserves Ember's polite mismatch-to-context register without reusing the style-bible example wording.
Noor is identified on the page by her visible badge at the body-discovery beat, and the later family-notification passage names her again before the chapter leaves institutional aftermath.

Chapter 18's copied Ember calibration line is removed. The packet's broader manuscript-wide echo requirement refers to another chapter that was outside this turn's allowed inputs and edit scope, so cross-chapter alignment could not be verified here.
The chapter was rewritten without em dashes, bringing prose-line em-dash density below the packet threshold.
Fatima is now placed inside the third-floor shared bathroom, consistent with the continuity sheet, rather than appearing to be on the stairwell landing.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 18 is rebuilt as an explicitly sourced aftermath coda. The rewrite replaces omniscient outsider reportage with document-grounded observation, corrects the body-count arithmetic and inbound-only relay continuity, identifies Noor on the page before the corporate fallout, restores Fatima to the third-floor bathroom, replaces the copied Ember line, and removes the chapter's em-dash-heavy prose pattern.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=7, PARTIAL=1, UNRESOLVED=0).
